Hello everybody and welcome back. This is Ace number three, our playbook called the Tyler Durden follow up. So this is something you're going to use. Specifically, if you're doing enrollment call sales call strategy calls, whatever you call them in your business, this is something you are going to be implementing. So a little bit of background on why it is called the Tyler Durden playbook. Fight Club fans may get a little bit of an inkling, but it's based on this scene with the famous, what was it, a convenience store clerk named Raymond K. Hessell. So Tyler Durden kind of brings him back to the back of the parking lot and questions him and grills him on what his dreams were before he ended up being a grocery store clerk or a convenience store clerk, sorry. And he talks about wanting to be a vet, right? That's his dream. And Tyler Durden with a gun to his head says, I know where you live. I'm keeping your license and I'm going to check in on you, Mr. Raymond K. Hessell in three months and then six months and then a year. And if you aren't back in school on your way to being a vet, you will be dead. So that's a little bit harsh. We're certainly not using that language. But all that aside, right? Tyler Durden's follow up game is pretty on point. That's three follow ups. He's promising Raymond K. Hessell to make sure that he is living according to his dreams, the things he's committed to, the things that he has said gives his life meaning and value. And Tyler is not going to let him off the hook. So a little bit similar, but obviously taking the good out of that while stripping away all the things that are illegal, not to mention unethical. So the Tyler Durden playbook is at its core about creating follow up processes, keyword processes in the spirit of what we've been talking about, which is the 100% close. So let's revisit that concept. Anyone who is on the crux of a purchase and who has ascended to this container with a clear invitation should be eventually held accountable to a binary yes or no. So that's what we refer to as closing a decision loop. And the more decision loops you close, the more yeses and knows you'll get. But beyond that, the more clarity you're going to have on every pre-customer in your ecosystem and the more clarity everyone who has actually invested all that time, attention and energy in trying to decide on your program, they're going to have that clarity to move forward as well. So that's what we're doing here with the Tyler Durden playbook. We're going to focus on closing the loop on every enrollment conversation we have, strategy calls, sales calls, whatever you call them that you've had with pre-customers. So the starting point for this playbook is that an invitation has already been made. So you've already had a conversation with them and they haven't made the purchase. So they weren't a green light to move forward, but they haven't been a clear overt no either. So they're not a red light. They're in this yellow light zone and they could either go through or they could stop, right? Do nothing. They kind of have to make a decision to move forward in some kind of direction. So how could this yellow light be communicated in ways that you may identify it right away when it's coming up, right? So this is essentially going to be any form of not being ready. So it might come off as not right now, maybe later. First I'm going to try X, right? I have these ideas of other things I can implement to try to get to my goal. And then we'll see how that's going and maybe check in later. That's how it could come across a lot of the times. You'll also hear a lot of this version. I'm already doing Y, right? I already am taking this course, taking this program. I want to see how that plays out first. Sometimes you'll get the delay. Let me know next time you're doing this. Other times I'm going to review my options and let you know, keep me posted on future offers. It doesn't feel like the right time. I need more time to think about it. Of course that's a very common one, an understandable one as well. I want to, but I don't have the funds right now. Another common one. And I'd love to, but I've got so many other courses and programs around this. I don't know if I could do another one. So these are all variations of a not being ready or a not right now that aren't an overt. No. Now, when all this stuff comes up, typically there are two responses that the salesperson or the enrollment specialist will offer, right? The overly aggressive response who will use every persuasion trick in his bag, you know, urgency. I can only hold this deal until the end of the call. After that, I'm going to remove this discount, right? Then there's fear mongering over potential consequences of exaggerating all the damage, all the things I will decay unless this is addressed right now. And it's true. There may be some things that get worse if not addressed. But in this case of the overly aggressive, they will certainly exaggerate that to the max to really try to milk all the fear out of you and turn that into compliance at the end of the call. There could certainly be some form of gaslighting by reminding the prospect of micro commitments they've made, right? So typical thing that you'll see and you'll recognize quite specifically in this type of salesperson is they may ask you earlier in the call, right? Like, you know, is this a dream that you're committed to doing, right? On the scale of 1 to 10, how committed are you to achieving this goal, right? And you'll give it 10 because it's kind of safe to do so in that moment. And they're kind of on your side and it feels real to you. And then they'll totally mirror that back, right? They'll obviously add, you know, this mega investment to the mix, which totally changes the context, right? And they're also going to create a false equivalency with you achieving that desire and goal as well as their program being the only way to achieve it, right? So a bit of a false equivalency there, a bit of gaslighting in terms of telling you what you wanted, even though you never said, I wanted and I'm committed and I'm a 10 on 10 to investing $10,000 right now taking this program working with you specifically and achieving that goal. So false equivalencies, certainly a form of gaslighting in this scenario. So this is what you'll typically get when you're on the phone with an over-aggressive, high-pressured person. And it usually comes from this disguised insecurity, right? This fear that if they let you off the hook, if they let you off the phone, that'll be that, right? All these things are essentially bailout and the paycheck is gone as soon as you get off the phone. So that's an insecurity that could easily and quickly be resolved and turned into a bit more of a gentle spaciousness when you have the adequate follow-up processes, which we're going to show you how to do with the Tyler Dyrd and Playbook. On the flip side, you also get people who are a bit overly passive, right? Who will get those forms of maybe's not right now's resistance as we just kind of outlined there. And instead of still holding some kind of a leadership frame, right? Getting resourceful, looking at different options, getting specific and unpacking what those concerns are, they'll immediately just kind of fold and collapse and start orienting around that uncertainty, right? And bailing out on the potential relationship prematurely instead of exploring what a new set of possibilities might look like. So I used to fall under this camp, right? I would just collapse when any of these 10 things came up. The call was done for me, right? And I kind of like masked that as like, it just wasn't the right fit, right? It wasn't, you know, the right time for them all good. But that's not necessarily true. There may be some validity to that. And at the same time, it certainly doesn't hurt to empower a decision and be as welcoming of a clear no as a clear yes. And that is what the spirit of the Tyler Dyrd and follow-up process is all about. So it's a follow-up system that you can use time and space, right? Allow them to get off that line. Allow them to explore those options. And this happens towards both your advantages, right? Through that, through allowing them that spaciousness and trusting that there's a follow-up process, you build greater trust, you build greater accountability, and you give them the opportunity, most importantly, to make empowered evidence-based decisions instead of decisions based out of fear mongering, exaggerated consequences, and essentially how they're making a terrible decision if they don't buy from you right now, right? So that is compliance that is not an empowered, an empowered close to that container. And to start a new relationship, it's actually a very awful way to start a new container, especially one where they all of a sudden now need to trust you on the other side of it to coach them and lead them when you didn't even demonstrate that on the sales call. You just forced compliance by fear mongering and exaggerating consequence. So all that said, for any maybe later response, all the ones we outline, any others that feel similar to the ones we shared there, it's extremely important for both of you that one, their wish for more time is ultimately honored, respected, and unpacked and made specific. What do they really mean by needing more time? What are they going to be doing with that time? What do they mean by saying, I'm going to try a few other things and then follow up in the future? What do they mean by this investment doesn't work for me right now? The specifics of it, because the more specific you get, the more clear you are on what to follow up with next in that spirit, you help them get clear on what their alternate self created path is. So if they say, I'm going to try things out on my own, right and follow up. Awesome. Quite specifically, right? What are you going to be doing in these next two or three months? In the spirit of achieving the school, if they're not clear on it, it's not the right time to get off the call. It's not going to be in service to them. Of course, it's not going to be in service to you or the relationship that you're trying to build with them. If you drop it prematurely. So that's something we want to help them get clear on as well as us get clear on while we are still in this position of leading and coaching them. It's also important that they know in the interim if they've chosen the self created path, they're taking accountability and responsible for their success. And then finally, get permission, right? Their goals are important and you could certainly get permission to check in on their progress later to make sure it's going smoothly and close that loop. Cool. And the important thing to remember in all of this is that a knot right now in any of these forms we discussed is still your closest pre customer, right? And should be treated as such rather than just kind of like brushed aside, tossed aside as you hunt for more leads. Why? Because they've stated the game that they're going to be playing solo, right? They've suggested a self created path. They know they're responsible for it. And then one to three months or whatever timeframe is appropriate for your industry or your project that you'd be helping them with, they will have clear empirical, indisputable evidence, not fear, mongering or for fear based conjecture of whether or not they truly would have benefited from your help or not. And this is so massive, right? And that decision becomes either confirmed or invalidated based on empirical evidence from a self created path that they suggested they'd be trying in the meantime. Cool. So what you're going to do is you're going to mirror back their self created path and get permission to follow up. That's going to be the key thing you do after you've helped them unpack what they're going to be doing in the meantime. You're going to mirror it back like this and get that permission to follow up. So what might that, what might, what might that look like? So if you are a weight loss coach, for example, you may go, you may get some version of their self created path and mirror it back like this. Okay. So what I'm hearing is that now isn't really the right time for you because you're confident that you could commit yourself to working out about four times per week using these three kettlebell videos on YouTube and that you expect you'll lose those 13 pounds and have more energy and confidence within 60 days. And they may say yes. And if you believe there's no danger in them attempting that solo, you can reply with awesome. Well, I certainly appreciate your confidence and your commitment in yourself. As you know, it can be a challenging path and there is a bit of amount of risk of falling off of that plan. So as we talked about, a lot of folks in your situation will get bogged down from just that lack of peer direction and accountability and wondering if they're what they're doing. It's even really working, which tends to hurt motivation, but it does sound like you really are ready to give it an honest shot. So why don't we, where did my slide go? There we go. But it sounds like you're ready to give it an honest shot and see what you can do. So I totally commend you for that. It's a cool if I follow up in about a month to make sure you're on track. So there we go. We're mirroring back essentially what they've stated they're going to be doing and we're asking for permission to follow up to make sure they are staying on track with their stated goals. One more example, one where it may not be as ethical for you to let them off the hook if you have really real concern that going at it solo can run a little bit more risky. It's certainly well within your compassionate leadership to say so. So if you're a holistic nutritionist working with someone who has intense chronic fatigue symptoms that is maybe really cutting into their productivity as an entrepreneur and the income they are able to generate because of their low energy levels, you may say something like great. So what I'm understanding is that you believe you can overcome your chronic fatigue symptoms by cutting out your nightly red wine and committing to your own self care routine. And they'll say yes, right? And that's indeed what they're going to be doing. And if you disagree, you can say I totally respect your choice, but can I be direct and honest for a second? And if they give you permission on that, say in all honesty, I'd be acting a bit unethically if I didn't at least give you a gentle warning that I have a strong conviction that based on what you share, there may be underlying causes that can't be addressed with a simple self care ritual on its own. Based on my experience, there are many potential underlying hormonal balances that require a more thorough approach. Would you be open to me checking in in two weeks from now just to see how you're doing and make sure you haven't regressed in that time? So that's something you may share if you really don't believe in the effectiveness of their self created path. Sometimes when you do this, it may actually reignite the conversation. They may actually want to re-explore a more solid proven layered expert driven protocol that you provide. Cool. So this is the process for the Tyler Durden call audit. So this is something you're going to be doing at the conclusion of every sales call you have. It's a way to really kind of map out your thoughts and create your re-entry path while it's still fresh in your head. So six sections. Section one, there's some basic information you're going to fill out. We have a walkthrough below this on details of how to do that. Section two, if a yes, the things you chart and document here are things that you can certainly use for reverse engineering. What should be a lead? So lead score signal. What should you be trying to signal or create opportunities for your prospects to signal? What has been true of your best customers? What has been true of those who have said yes? These are things you may want to be able to mirror back in your marketing and your lead signaling. Section three, if a no, we have some guidelines of how to process that out, including some ways that it might actually make sense to follow up with those nos with new opportunities. The three sections I want to really focus on here as it pertains to the Tyler Durden process, the follow up process, is we're going to be addressing the maybes or not right now as we spoke about earlier in this lesson. We're going to create your re-entry path and finally your airport moment, which we're going to get into in a second. So below this video, you should see a whole walkthrough on all these sections with some detailed breakdowns, but we're just going to quickly wrap up on what is the art of the re-entry path. So your follow up, the one you make with them in that agreed upon timeframe should accurately mirror back their stated goal, their desire, their problem, and how they specifically chose to address it. And there's typically going to be three scenarios you're following up with, right? People who are doing something else instead. So those are the self directed actions we just spoke about. People who just needed more time to consider options and discuss with their partner or spouse. So we're going to follow up with those. And there's also going to be scenarios where finances were a challenge and you may want to follow up with opportunities that may be more aligned for them. So what are the possibilities for a re-entry path? It could be the exact same offer with just are you ready now, right? Has your self created path been effective or ineffective? It could be with a new offer or opportunity at a lower price point. If your maybe or not right now is about the investment just being inaccessible at this time. You want to take note of and circle back with a new offer that may be a more reasonable or appropriate investment for them. And then finally it could be a new offer or opportunity that now caters more to his or her true need, right? If you have refined your offer, refined your package, refined your program based on feedback and that feedback or those new additions or upgrades, sorry, sync up well with some of the concerns they may have had on that call. That could be a perfect time to come back and propose that new path together. So last thing I want to do before we wrap up is the airport moment. So what is the airport moment? So if you had to go for broke, you had to put all your cards on the table and if you truly feel like they're making a big mistake in saying no, you can reach out and reply to them within 48 hours with that go for broke airport moment. This is inspired by all those cheesy 90s rom-coms or sitcoms where there was that woman who was about to leave for another town and this dude would just like rush to the airport red in a cab and then he would rush and storm past the security gates and stop her from making what he believed to be the biggest mistake of her life, which was probably not true, but that's the story for another day. But yeah, that's essentially the airport moment. So this is the one who watched television or movies in the 90s probably knows exactly what I'm talking about. So what is the general guideline for the airport moment? So this is something you're going to do more in B2C, fitness coaches, relationship dating, ironically, since we're talking about airport moments, spiritual coaching, emotional, mindset coaching, things of that nature. This is probably less appropriate in B2B settings and any kind of settings where there are multiple decision makers, marketing services, business consulting, probably not an appropriate place to do it. And if you are going to do it, once again, same rules apply, you're going to mirror back the goal that they had, you're going to mirror back the objection or reason for saying not right now. And then you're really just going to put your cards on the table, right? And with a very clear I want to work with you and I believe we can actually achieve this goal together, right? You're going to go with that massive confident appeal, right? You're confident in them achieving it. Your desire for working with them, why that's true. And then you may suggest getting on another call to see what you could do to make it work. So what might this look like again, a holistic health coach who might look like this. Hey Emma, thanks again for taking the time to connect this week and for sharing so bravely. I'll be honest. I don't mind a lot these past few days. I have a ton of admiration and appreciation for your goal of losing 15 pounds a month and reclaiming your energy power or confidence before your wedding. Yay. I understand that finances were a bit of a challenge, but when it comes to your goal, I know it can be done. I personally thought we had a great vibe. You're someone I'd love to work with. And with your amazing energy and fierce commitment, I think we can be celebrating your new target weight well before your June wedding. If you feel similarly, would you be open to just a 20 minute chat tomorrow to see what we can do to make it work and move forward with confidence? So that is your go for broke airport moment. You're putting your cards on the table. You're really stating your genuine desire to work with them. Why that is true, what you appreciated about them and you're putting confidence in the goal that they wish to achieve. Cool. So that's all I got for you on this one. Next up below this video, you should see the whole Tyler Durden call audit workbook walkthrough. We're going to walk through all the six sections of that that you're going to want to process. You're going to want to take about 15 to 20 minutes to process that after every sales call. And that's essentially going to tell you what are your follow up steps with these people who are maybe or not right now. And then we're going to walk you through how to set up the automations for this follow up process. Right. So you're going to have these notes of what that reentry path is. Then you're going to set a reminder of when to follow up. And then that's just going to ping you going to show up in your inbox. And you're going to be able to close all these decision loops. And remember, when you close these decision loops, you'll get more knows, you'll get more yeses and overall, your conversions and your revenues are only going to go up through doing this right for what I consider to be a minimal time investment for doing it. So if you are doing enrollment calls, even just a few per week, even just a few per month, this is something that can have a profound and immediate ROI on the first sales call you process and follow up on. So that's all I got for you. I'll see you in the next video. Have a great rest of the day, I guess. I don't know why I said that, but I said that. Take care guys.
